sales.description: ISLANDS off TROAS, Tenedos. Circa 210/05-191 BC. AR Tetradrachm (29.5mm, 17.04 g, 12h). In the name and types of Alexander III of Macedon. Head of Herakles right, wearing lion skin / AΛEΞANΔPOY, Zeus Aëtophoros seated left; labrys in left field. Meadows, Double-Axe, Group 5a, dies O18/R47, 128 (this coin); Thompson, Alexandrine, Group 3, 19–20; Price 2074 (Miletos or Mylasa); ANS inv. 1944.100.32225 (same obv. die); Prospero 311 (same obv. die). sales.description: Macedonian Kingdom. Antigonos Gonatas, 277-239 BC. Silver Tetradrachm (17.14g), 27mm. Issue in the name and type of Alexander the Great, struck in Pella (Amphipolis) c. 276/5-273/2 BC Head of young Herakles in lion skin headdress. Reverse:  Zeus enthroned left holding eagle and scepter. Monogram to l. and under throne. HGC Vol. 3, 1040; Price 574-603. NGC graded Choice XF, Strike: 5/5, Surface: 3/5.
